title: lichen-oscore protect/unprotect return heapless::Vec while other codecs take output buffer parameter
description:
SCHC, CoAP, SenML, and Link codec functions take an output buffer:
- compress(packet, out: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, SchcError>
- CoapBuilder::new(out: &mut [u8], ...) -> Result<Self, CoapError>
- encode(records, out: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, CborError>
- LichenFrame::write_to(&self, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, FrameError>

But lichen-oscore/src/lib.rs returns owned heapless::Vec:
  pub fn protect_request(&mut self, ...) -> Result<(heapless::Vec<u8, 280>, heapless::Vec<u8, 16>), OscoreError>

What goes wrong: Callers cannot reuse existing buffers for OSCORE operations, forcing allocation of fixed-size heapless::Vec on every call. In embedded contexts this may cause stack pressure. The API is harder to compose with the rest of the stack which uses &mut [u8] output buffers.
